title: Fix subclass with rand_mode(0) getting randomized (#7376)
body:
## Summary

This patch fixes `rand_mode(0)` on a member variable inside a
globally-constrained sub-object being ignored during the parent's
`randomize()` call -- the variable was still randomized and written
back by the solver. Fixes #7376.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
class inner extends base_obj;
  rand logic [31:0] value;
  constraint c { value < 100; }
  function void set_rand_mode(bit mode);
    value.rand_mode(mode);
    c.constraint_mode(mode);
  endfunction
endclass

class outer extends base_obj;
  rand inner fld;
  function void build();
    fld = new;
  endfunction
endclass

initial begin
  outer o = new;
  o.build();
  o.fld.set_rand_mode(0);       // disable fld.value
  pre_val = o.fld.value;
  void'(o.randomize());
  assert(o.fld.value == pre_val); // FAILS on master
end
```

On master:
```
%Error: got='hafed exp='h0  (fld.value changed despite rand_mode(0))
```

## Changes

- Added file-scope `getRandModeVarFromClass()` that walks the class
  extends chain to locate the `__Vrandmode` variable via
  `AstClass::user2p()`, which is only set on the root class where
  the variable was created.
- Replaced direct `varClassp->user2p()` lookups in
  `ConstraintExprVisitor` (constraint expression formatting and
  `write_var` disable/enable generation) with the new helper.
- Removed `RandomizeVisitor::getRandModeVar()` -- unified into the
  single file-scope function used by both visitors.

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_constraint_global_randmode_subobj.{v,py}` --
  UVM-like hierarchy where `rand_mode(0)` on a sub-object member must
  prevent randomization during the parent's `randomize()`. Tests both
  disable and re-enable paths.

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
